canonical_definition: Across the provided sources, Oxford-IIIT Pets is consistently used as a benchmark for image or visual classification tasks. The prevailing usage is to employ it for evaluating a model's prediction correctness, as noted in multiple papers. A smaller set of studies uses it to assess more specific capabilities, including few-shot learning performance, as well as personalized and generalized performance in federated learning contexts. The benchmark is operationalized in experimental setups for both zero-shot and linear probe evaluations. One paper further specifies the task as the fine-grained classification of pet breeds. In line with this usage, the dataset is directly cited as a way to measure the behavior of image classification.
papers: Privacy-Preserving Personalized Federated Prompt Learning for Multimodal Large Language Models
Weighted Multi-Prompt Learning with Description-free Large Language Model Distillation
Beyond Accuracy: Ensuring Correct Predictions With Correct Rationales
IPO: Interpretable Prompt Optimization for Vision-Language Models

definitions.text: An image classification benchmark used for evaluating prediction correctness.
definitions.paper: Beyond Accuracy: Ensuring Correct Predictions With Correct Rationales

definitions.text: A dataset for fine-grained classification of pet breeds, used to evaluate few-shot learning performance.
definitions.paper: Weighted Multi-Prompt Learning with Description-free Large Language Model Distillation

relationships.paper: A Grounded Typology of Word Classes
